title: Bulk material conveyor
abstract: A bulk material shipping conveyor including a support assembly, a housing connected to the support assembly, a conveyor belt assembly partially positioned in the housing, a motor assembly coupled to the conveyor belt assembly, a hopper assembly including a receiving assembly including a plurality of walls and at least one movable flap extending above at least one of the plurality of walls, a gate assembly including a door movable between an open position and a closed position, and a transition assembly including a plurality of spaced apart conveyor supports, wherein at least one of the spaced apart conveyor supports is rotated relative to a longitudinal axis of the housing.
claims: Claims CLAIMS The claims are as follows: 1. A bulk material conveyor comprising: a support assembly; a housing connected to the support assembly; a conveyor belt assembly partially positioned in the housing; a motor assembly coupled to the conveyor belt assembly; a hopper assembly including a receiving assembly including a plurality of walls, and a movable flap extending above one of the plurality of walls; a gate assembly including a door movable between an open position and a closed position; and a transition assembly including a plurality of spaced apart conveyor supports, wherein at least one of the spaced apart conveyor supports is rotated relative to a longitudinal axis of the housing. 2. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , which includes a fork tine receiver assembly including two rear end fork tine receivers connected to the support assembly, and two side fork tine receivers connected to the support assembly. 3. The bulk material conveyor of claim 2, wherein the two rear end fork tine receivers are parallel to a longitudinal axis of the bulk material conveyor, and wherein the two side fork tine receivers are parallel to a transverse axis of the bulk material conveyor. 4. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , which includes a diverter assembly connected to the hopper assembly. 5. The bulk material conveyor of claim 4, wherein the diverter assembly includes a grate positioned in the hopper assembly, and a material diverter extending upwardly from the grate. 6. The bulk material conveyor of claim 5, wherein the material diverter is substantially centered on the grate. 7. The bulk material conveyor of claim 5, wherein the material diverter includes an angled front face, an angled rear face, an angled first side face, and an angled second side face. 8. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , wherein the hopper assembly includes a support structure including an upright support member, an angled support member, and a lateral connection member. 9. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , wherein the receiving assembly of the hopper assembly includes a front wall, a rear wall, a first side wall, and a second side wall. 10. The bulk material conveyor of claim 9, wherein the receiving assembly of the hopper assembly includes one of: a first side flap movably connected to the first side wall; and a second side flap movably connected to the second side wall. 11. The bulk material conveyor of claim 9, wherein the receiving assembly of the hopper assembly includes: a first side flap movably connected to the first side wall; and a second side flap movably connected to the second side wall. 12. The bulk material conveyor of claim 9, wherein the receiving assembly of the hopper assembly includes: a front flap extending upwardly from the front wall; a rear flap extending upwardly from the rear wall; a first side flap movably connected to the first side wall; and a second side flap movably connected to the second side wall. 13. The bulk material conveyor of claim 12, wherein the receiving assembly of the hopper assembly includes one or more stabilizer rods connected to both the front flap and the rear flap. 14. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , wherein the transition assembly includes: a first lateral end support; and a second lateral end support, wherein the plurality of conveyor supports are connected between the first lateral end support and the second lateral end support. 15. The bulk material conveyor of claim 14, wherein the transition assembly includes one or more rollers coupled to the second lateral end support. 16. The bulk material conveyor of claim 1 , wherein the transition assembly includes: first, second, third, and fourth conveyor supports extending perpendicular to a transverse axis of the bulk material conveyor, wherein the first and fourth conveyor supports are partially rotated about the longitudinal axis of the housing such that material dropped onto the transition assembly is diverted toward a center line of the conveyor belt assembly when the material contacts the first and fourth conveyor supports.
